title: The Movie of the Year Is Here: ‘Boys State’
format: Sean and Amanda call the Apple TV+ documentary the best movie they've seen this year, then talk to directors Jesse Moss and Amanda McBaine about casting four 17-year-olds and shooting a mock election in six days.

segments.heading: Boys State
segments.note: dir. Jesse Moss and Amanda McBaine

films.title: Boys State
films.note: A24 and Apple bought it out of Sundance: four Texas teenagers run a mock gubernatorial campaign at the American Legion's weeklong civics program, and the tactics get real fast.

segments.heading: In conversation
segments.guest: Jesse Moss and Amanda McBaine on

films.title: Boys State
films.note: Cast three of the film's four central figures before the event even started, then found Renee Otero on day two, minutes before he gave the speech that put him in the movie.

films.title: The War Room
films.note: Moss calls it foundational to his own career: the film that convinced him to trade politics for documentary work.

films.title: Primary
films.note: The Robert Drew film they cite for Al Maysles's shot of JFK entering a room in Wisconsin.

films.title: Crisis: Behind a Presidential Commitment
films.note: Drew's film shot both sides of a standoff, JFK and George Wallace: the kind of access on each side of a conflict that Moss and McBaine also got at Boys State.
